tagline: The CPTO's playbook for agentic AI: patterns, anti-patterns, decision trees, and procurement-grade checklists for shipping reliable agents in production.
definition: Production agents fail because the system around the model is weak. The seven surfaces of a reliable agent system spell AGENTIC.

surfaces.name: Autonomy
surfaces.blurb: How much the agent decides for itself, and where the workflow rails are.

surfaces.name: Goals
surfaces.blurb: Design specs and structured outputs that pin down what the agent is for.

surfaces.name: Evaluation
surfaces.blurb: The eval-driven loop and the observability that proves the agent works.

surfaces.name: Networks
surfaces.blurb: The protocol stack that connects agents to tools and to each other (Skills/MCP/A2A).

surfaces.name: Trust
surfaces.blurb: Security, the threat model, the EU AI Act, and the anti-patterns that get you breached.

surfaces.name: Identity
surfaces.blurb: Who the agent is, what it is allowed to do, and how it authenticates.

surfaces.name: Cost
surfaces.blurb: The cost stack and model selection that keep the bill survivable.

capabilityLadder.name: Suggest-only
capabilityLadder.criteria: Drafts and suggestions; a human takes every consequential action.

capabilityLadder.name: Act-with-approval
capabilityLadder.criteria: Agent proposes actions; a human approves each before execution.

capabilityLadder.name: Act-with-guardrails
capabilityLadder.criteria: Agent acts inside hard, pre-execution policy limits; humans review after the fact.

capabilityLadder.name: Act-autonomously
capabilityLadder.criteria: Agent acts unattended within a bounded domain; HITL only on flagged exceptions.

capabilityLadder.name: Self-directed
capabilityLadder.criteria: Agent sets and revises its own goals across domains without scoped human oversight.

socialPreviewText: AGENTIC: the seven surfaces of a reliable agent system. A vendor-neutral, CPTO-grade playbook for shipping agents in production.
